ws9_artifacts.auth_mode: Mode B (browser login) â€” verification class E2E. The controlled-print route's server client is @supabase/ssr cookie-based, so Mode C Bearer does NOT authenticate it; only a real browser session does. Login via the dev password form at /signin (DevLoginForm, dev-only; magic-link /auth/login cannot password-login). Seed operator pitboss@dev.local is staff_role=admin on SEED_CASINO_ID â€” satisfies the route role gate (pit_boss|admin) AND same-casino P0003 instrument resolution. Controlled print driven through the authenticated browser request context (page.request) so cookies carry.
ws9_artifacts.gate: e2e-write-path GREEN â€” genuine local run (NOT fabricated). 1 passed. Asserts HTTP 200 + outcome.status='submitted' + outcome.failure=null + replayed=false, then service-role reads print_attempt: casino_id=SEED_CASINO_ID (context-derived), operator_id=SEED_STAFF_ID, instrument_kind='promo_coupon', instrument_ref=coupon id, result_status='submitted', failure_domain=NULL (Â§7a), receipt_document_hash present, reprint_of=NULL.
ws9_artifacts.run_recipe: Default playwright.config.ts force-loads REMOTE .env.local (override:true) which lacks print_attempt -> PGRST202; this write-path test MUST run local. Recipe (README): (1) local supabase up + print migrations applied; (2) LOYALTY_PRINT_AGENT_PORT=9787 npx tsx e2e/loyalty-printing/support/loopback-print-agent-server.ts; (3) dev server on :3100 with local supabase env + LOYALTY_PRINT_AGENT_URL=http://127.0.0.1:9787 + LOYALTY_PRINT_TARGET_ID=loopback-cups; (4) export local supabase env in the test shell, then `npx playwright test --config ./playwright.loyalty-printing.config.ts --reporter=list`.

(b) Pre-React-hydration submit caused a native GET leaking creds to URL â€” added a Next-hydration wait (window.next) before click. ws8_artifacts.dup1_d_nuance: WS8(d) two-concurrent-first_print asserts the DETERMINISTIC physical-once boundary per ws6_artifacts.exactly_once_nuance: exactly ONE print_attempt row (DB UNIQUE single-flight), both calls resolve same printAttemptId, and the agent jobKey dedupe -> a single distinct spooler jobId (one physical document). The literal 'agent submitJob spy == 1' is asserted on the SEQUENTIAL replay path (case a), where adapter-once is deterministic; under true Promise.all concurrency both peers may observe 'requested' and reach the adapter, so call-count==1 is NOT asserted there (would be flaky) â€” the audit single-flight + jobKey dedupe is the real physical-once boundary.
ws8_artifacts.pre_existing_lint_debt: NOT introduced by WS8. WS3/WS4/WS5 source files (renderers/schemas/templates/dtos/mappers) + the WS2 exemplar test carry prettier-formatting + no-dto-type-assertions errors when linted ad-hoc. The official `npm run lint` uses `git ls-files` so it SKIPS these UNTRACKED files (hence WS3-5 'eslint PASS' claims). My boundary rule produced ZERO false positives. Left frozen-source untouched (out of WS8 scope); my new test files were prettier-formatted clean.

ws7_artifacts.dec_004: auto_attempt auto-print DROPPED: handleFulfillmentReady no longer calls print(); IssuanceResultPanel no longer auto-fires a print (onFulfillmentReady retained as a NON-printing notification, fires once on fresh issuance). ws7_artifacts.dec_008: No re-drive Retry affordance. After a terminal outcome, the only resubmission path is a nonce-bearing Reprint (useControlledPrint.reprint mints crypto.randomUUID() reprintNonce + reprintOf=prior attempt.printAttemptId). For outcome=unknown the Reprint button is gated behind an AlertDialog duplicate-risk acknowledgement ('Reprint anyway'). HTTP/transport errors (state='error', e.g. 503 agent unconfigured) surface a 'Try again' first_print (no terminal row exists yet â€” not a re-drive).
ws7_artifacts.terminology_7a: PrintOutcomeBadge vocabulary bounded to submitted|failed|unknown. submitted copy = 'Sent to printer â€” not yet confirmed printed' (does NOT imply physical completion). No 'printed'/'completed'/'acknowledged'/'device'/PrinterFault vocabulary. failure copy maps bounded transport/render codes only.

ws6_artifacts.exactly_once_nuance: FLAGGED FOR WS8: physical-print exactly-once is guaranteed at THREE layers: (1) HTTP Idempotency-Key edge single-flight for double-click, (2) DB UNIQUE(casino_id,idempotency_key) insert-or-return-prior, (3) WS5 agent jobKey dedupe (same instrumentâ†’same docâ†’one spool). Terminal write is single-flight via P0100. CAVEAT: the frozen WS2 rpc_request returns a bare row without an 'inserted' flag, so WS6 cannot distinguish INSERT-winner from a concurrent peer that also sees 'requested'; under TRUE concurrency both may call adapter.print, but the agent jobKey dedupe collapses to ONE physical spool and only one transition wins. WS8(d) 'adapter spy == 1 under concurrent first_print' should assert against the AGENT submitJob spy (the real physical-once boundary) â€” the literal adapter.print-once holds on the normal sequential path. No WS2 amendment made (frozen, 14/14).
